bbc-four.title: University Challenge
bbc-four.desc: The second round continues, with two more teams competing for a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

bbc-four.title: Only Connect
bbc-four.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. A team of three lovers of history square up to a trio of Welshmen devoted to their national game. They compete to draw together the connections between things which, at first glance, seem utterly random: Mao Zedong, Snow White, Tilda Swinton and Lindow Man.

bbc-four.title: Artsnight
bbc-four.desc: Carla Lane redefined British comedy drama in the 1970s with her unique brand of 'situation tragedy'. At a time when television writing was the preserve of middle-class men, she brought a convincing cast of working-class female characters to British screens. The daring honesty with which she told the stories of ordinary women revolutionised the roles available to actresses on TV and blazed a trail for the screenwriters following in her footsteps. This special edition of Artsnight delves into the BBC's archive and brings together rarely seen interviews in which writer Carla Lane discusses her life and work, while Carla's son Carl offers personal insights into his mother's career and legacy. With contributions from the Liver Birds - Polly James and Nerys Hughes - and Geoffrey Palmer, the long-suffering husband to Ria in Butterflies.

bbc-four.title: Butterflies
bbc-four.desc: Ria has enough to cope with: there is her husband Ben, who has his own hang-ups and her sons Adam and Russell, who are the cause of most of them. Then suddenly there's Leonard.

bbc-four.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
bbc-four.desc: She began the century that bears her name a princess and ended it as an empress. Queen Victoria ruled one of the most powerful empires in world history during a century of staggering change - for both good and bad. But it was Victorian women who were at the forefront of the fight against its excesses and inequalities, who campaigned for the rights for ordinary people in marriage, education, medicine and the vote.

bbc-four.title: Muhammad Ali
bbc-four.desc: Born in Louisville, Kentucky in 1942, Cassius Clay rises from the world of amateur boxing to win gold at the 1960 Olympic Games. Following his Olympic success, Clay turns professional and moves to Miami to train with Angelo Dundee, sharpening his boxing skills and honing his genius for self-promotion.

bbc-four.title: Muhammad Ali
bbc-four.desc: Cassius Clay is determined to become the youngest ever champion by taking on the ‘unbeatable’ Sonny Liston, the reigning world heavyweight champion. Clay needs to fight other contenders first, including a famous bout with Henry Cooper in London. Meanwhile, he quietly joins the Nation of Islam and becomes a confidant of Malcolm X.

bbc-four.title: Muhammad Ali
bbc-four.desc: Cassius Clay publicly joins the Nation of Islam and adopts the name Muhammad Ali. For three years, he dominates the heavyweight ranks, but in 1967 the US army extends the military draft, and Ali becomes eligible for service in Vietnam.

bbc-four.title: Muhammad Ali
bbc-four.desc: Muhammad Ali refuses induction into the US Army as a conscientious objector and becomes one of the most reviled men in America. Ali, stripped of his title, is convicted of draft evasion and forced into exile. In 1970, he triumphantly returns to the ring, but it’s clear that he has lost a step.

bbc-news.title: Talking Business
bbc-news.desc: Will the Iran war cut Europe’s dependence on energy imports? Soaring energy prices are hurting businesses and household budgets across Europe, so will this crisis change the continent's energy mix for good? Despite efforts to switch to renewables, Europe is still hugely reliant on imported oil and gas. The CEO of Europe’s biggest gas distributor, Italgas, Paolo Gallo explains how new technology can help and Ben Thompson also hears from EU energy commissioner Dan Jørgensen about concerns that moving to solar, wind and nuclear power could just shift Europe’s dependency to other parts of the world.

bbc-one.title: Morning Live
bbc-one.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

bbc-one.title: Doorbell Detectives
bbc-one.desc: Matt Allwright hears from Jane, who awoke in the middle of the night to discover two masked men breaking into her elderly parents' house. Former police inspector Anton Sullivan gives some top tips on what to do if you come home and find out you’ve been burgled. Matt also hears from Tami, whose doorbell captured the moment she took on a gang of wannabe bike thieves.

bbc-one.title: Homes Under the Hammer
bbc-one.desc: Two houses in Worksop and Wakefield are bought at auction by developers who plan to rent them out. Meanwhile in Dewsbury, a midwife and her builder husband buy a house that has not been lived in for a decade, but they have big plans to create a forever home for them and their four children.

bbc-one.title: Bargain Hunt
bbc-one.desc: A hundred years ago in Yorkshire, it wasn’t ketchup that people added to their food - it was a delicious locally made relish. By the turn of the 20th century, 30 million bottles of Yorkshire Relish were being made in Leeds each year and shipped around the world. Made of vinegar, sugar, fruits and 27 spices, it was the go-to sauce for generations, right up until the 1950s. In the city where it was made, Danny Sebastian savours a collection of advertising items that celebrate this once-popular condiment. If that doesn’t whet your appetite, enjoy two teams of bargain hunters as they scour the antiques stalls at Wetherby Racecourse. On the red team are twins and classic car fans Mike and Joe, who hope to breakdance their way to victory - as long as they don’t break the bank first! Against them are mum and daughter Sue and Sarah, who plan on using their cha-cha-charm to get a good price. But which team will be dancing all the way to a profit at Caroline Hawley’s auction?

bbc-one.title: BBC News at One
bbc-one.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

bbc-one.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
bbc-one.desc: Natalie discovers the power of caring volunteers, and how mind and body work together to help people recover and move forward.

bbc-one.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
bbc-one.desc: Robson is joined by his footballing hero, former England and Newcastle United captain Alan Shearer. The football superstar and Match of the Day pundit takes time out to go mackerel fishing off the Northumberland coast, where the pair spot a pod of dolphins. Robson takes Alan to Eshott Airfield near Morpeth to take to the skies in a 1940s Tiger Moth.

bbc-one.title: Escape to the Country
bbc-one.desc: After 30 years in what was meant to be a temporary home in the city of Stoke-on-Trent, today’s couple is finally ready to make their escape to Wiltshire to be closer to family. With a firm belief that separate bathrooms are the secret to a happy marriage, they turn to Ginny Buckley to find them their perfect property. Ginny takes a break from the house search to meet someone already living the Wiltshire country dream by creating jams, chutneys and pickles.

bbc-one.title: Garden Rescue
bbc-one.desc: Chris and Frances are in Leicester to help Hector and Claudia transform their blank new-build plot into a Cypriot cooking garden for a growing family. Uneven grass, a sloped area behind the garage and a bare brick wall have left the space difficult to use. The couple dream of a garden reflecting Hector’s Cypriot heritage and their shared love of food, with a dedicated outdoor cooking and dining area, alongside a vegetable patch for their first child, Arthur, to learn about growing food. They also want to retain a grassy area for their dog, Lilo, to play in. Claudia and Hector met at Bristol University eight years ago, and after marrying in 2023, they celebrated their honeymoon in Rhodes with a romantic cooking class in the mountains. Now they’re ready to bring their Mediterranean-inspired vision home. Can the Garden Rescue team turn this blank canvas into a flourishing Cypriot cooking garden on a £4,000 budget?

bbc-one.title: Bridge of Lies
bbc-one.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four – in this episode, friends Kenny, Nicola, Lyndsey and Craig – take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

bbc-one.title: Pointless
bbc-one.desc: The brainy quiz returns - but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Ria Lina. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

bbc-one.title: BBC News at Six
bbc-one.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

bbc-one.title: BBC London
bbc-one.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

bbc-one.title: The One Show
bbc-one.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

bbc-one.title: EastEnders
bbc-one.desc: Yolande helps Denise as she processes life-changing news, and Ian is blissfully unaware that his happy life is about to change.

bbc-one.title: Panorama
bbc-one.desc: Giving birth in the UK is getting more dangerous; the number of women dying in childbirth is up 20% in the last ten years. Now, in Nottingham, the largest-ever maternity inquiry is underway, reviewing the medical records of thousands of births for signs of avoidable harm. Reporting for Panorama, Michael Buchanan hears powerful testimonies: from mothers who weren’t listened to, staff who blew the whistle but were ignored, and from parents whose babies’ tragic deaths were dismissed as ‘just bad luck’. The programme also hears from the new chief executive of the Nottingham University Hospitals NHS Trust, who has promised change. As families unite to demand answers, Panorama investigates how systemic neglect, inadequate staffing and a reluctance to learn from mistakes have led to heartbreak.

bbc-one.title: Scam Interceptors
bbc-one.desc: The team take on a group of scammers pretending to be from the UK’s National Crime Agency and frightening their victims into believing they will be arrested if they do not prove their identity. They have locked on to a student in Preston who thinks her National Insurance number has been compromised. The scammers say the only way to fix things is to buy gift cards and hand over the numbers on them. As the hub team try to reach her by phone, the ground team rush to the nearby supermarket to stop her buying the gift cards in person. Can the teams intercept the scammers and their victim in time?

bbc-one.title: Dear England
bbc-one.desc: As Euro 2024 begins, England arrive as favourites for Southgate’s final tournament. Gareth is forced to make some challenging changes to the team and puts his trust in a new generation. When the team struggles unexpectedly and pressure mounts, Gareth wonders how his story will end.

bbc-one.title: BBC News at Ten
bbc-one.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

bbc-one.title: BBC London
bbc-one.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

bbc-one.title: Have I Got News for You
bbc-one.desc: Guest host Katherine Ryan, journalist Jo Coburn and comedian John Tothill join Ian and Paul as they delve into the news.

bbc-one.title: Football’s New Generation
bbc-one.desc: The intimate story of England's newest recruits, charting their rise from grassroots heroes to Europe's biggest stage. Featuring Cole Palmer, Kobbie Mainoo, Ollie Watkins and Marc Guehi, the documentary reveals their personal stories for the first time, with contributions from their loved ones, family and friends, as well as the veterans who mentored them to the world stage. The film charts their paths from humble beginnings, revealing for the first time the personal battles that culminated in a new breed of players representing England at Euro 2024.

bbc-one.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
bbc-one.desc: Zoe Ball follows in her ancestors’ footsteps in a journey that takes her from Scotland to Cornwall as she uncovers stories of resilience and the importance of family over place.

bbc-one.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
bbc-one.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

bbc-one.title: Morning Live
bbc-one.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

bbc-one.title: Doorbell Detectives
bbc-one.desc: When Shaz receives a package he didn’t order, he turns Doorbell Detective and discovers a man intercepting deliveries on his doorstep, pretending to be him. Former police inspector Anton Sullivan demonstrates a simple doorstep scam on pensioner Beryl and gives her some tips to avoid it happening. And what would you do if you caught a burglar in the act of robbing your house? That's what Doorbell Detective Ethan faced.

bbc-one.title: Homes Under the Hammer
bbc-one.desc: Martin meets someone in Devon who is trading his gym membership for the manual labour of property development! Jacqui is in Norfolk off the beaten path... well sort of. Dion's been put behind bars in Birkenhead.

bbc-one.title: Bargain Hunt
bbc-one.desc: Roo Irvine and the teams go bargain-hunting in Northamptonshire with experts Gary Pe and Tim Weeks before heading to auction in Derby. Roo also gets special access to the Royal Crown Derby porcelain factory to learn about centuries of craftsmanship.

bbc-one.title: BBC News at One
bbc-one.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

bbc-one.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
bbc-one.desc: Natalie experiences being older. Will it help in her placement in a care home for older residents?

bbc-one.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
bbc-one.desc: Robson joins singer KT Tunstall in her home territory of Fife as they explore the beaches of her childhood home near St Andrews. They merge their voices in a sea shanty workshop at Elie Beach with traditional singer Joss Cameron. The duo beachcomb for materials to make jewellery, and Robson tries land yachting and discovers a new passion.

bbc-one.title: Escape to the Country
bbc-one.desc: In glorious Gloucestershire, Nicki Chapman helps Peggy finally make her long-awaited escape from London. After years of dreaming, she’s ready to find a home that suits her and her crew – two dogs and a horse called Tom. Emotions are running high as Nicki lines up a selection of spectacular properties from where some of the county’s amazing views can be seen. Nicki also meets a pair of street food chefs who have swapped the city buzz for a slice of country living.

bbc-one.title: Garden Rescue
bbc-one.desc: Chris and Amelia are in Barnsley to transform Rea and her daughter India’s bland and unsafe patch into a boho-style garden influenced by their love of upcycling, crafting and all things rustic. India would love a fairy area, while mum Rea wants a space to relax in and socialise with friends. Can the team achieve all this on a £5,000 budget?

bbc-one.title: Bridge of Lies
bbc-one.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four – in this episode, friends Mohsin, Gus, Amir and Ayoub – take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

bbc-one.title: Pointless
bbc-one.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Ria Lina for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

bbc-one.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

bbc-one.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

bbc-one.title: The One Show
bbc-one.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

bbc-one.title: EastEnders
bbc-one.desc: Ian’s world comes crashing down. Max shocks Cindy.

bbc-one.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
bbc-one.desc: It's the semi-finals, and the three remaining designers head to Brighton and Hove Albion's football stadium to transform two hospitality boxes each - one for a player's family and the other for a corporate client. Top fan Norman Cook, aka Fatboy Slim, pops in for a visit, alongside players Georginio Rutter and Fran Kirby. There is a lot to juggle in this tough challenge, and a place in the final at stake.

bbc-one.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
bbc-one.desc: Strictly dancer Amy Dowden turns amateur sleuth, travelling to west Wales to see if she can discover the truth behind a rumour of murder in her family history.

bbc-one.title: BBC News at Ten
bbc-one.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

bbc-one.title: BBC London
bbc-one.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

bbc-one.title: Half Man
bbc-one.desc: 2014. Niall has built a new life, but old ties resurface when Mona reconnects with Ruben. Events spiral towards an intense and devastating conclusion.

bbc-one.title: Everest
bbc-one.desc: Two groups of mountaineers face a deadly descent from Everest when storm conditions take them by surprise. Based on the 1996 Everest disaster.

bbc-three.title: Stacey Dooley
bbc-three.desc: Spending time with people who regularly steal from shops, Stacey gets to grips with the shoplifting epidemic and what it’s like for the frontline staff trying to prevent thefts.

bbc-three.title: Just Act Normal
bbc-three.desc: A darkly comic drama about three stubbornly optimistic siblings with a terrible secret. When their mum seemingly goes missing, Tiana, Tionne and Tanika will do anything to keep it quiet so that they can stay together as a family. But feistily resilient and fiercely loyal as they are, can they really outwit the authorities and carry on life under the radar? Tiana, the eldest, takes charge, determined to hold the household together until she turns 18 and can legally keep her siblings under one roof. But keeping their lives on track without a parent is a battle she is barely equipped for - especially as the lies pile up, the bills go unpaid and the world around them starts closing in. Things get complicated when Tiana is forced to allow her mum’s hapless drug dealer - Dr Feelgood – to move in with them after he realises that they are home alone. It’s an unholy union. Feelgood might be able to bring money and even a sense of security into their little flat, but he is unpredictable and chaotic, and Tionne holds him responsible for their mother’s disappearance. Furious at Tiana for letting the 'enemy' into their home, Tionne becomes hellbent on getting rid of Feelgood for ever.

bbc-three.title: Just Act Normal
bbc-three.desc: The siblings’ secret’s exposed. Tiana plans their escape but fails and is forced to confront their estranged dad. Feelgood offers to help, but there's a catch - he wants to move in.

bbc-three.title: The Trials of Pa Salieu
bbc-three.desc: After prison, Coventry musician Pa Salieu returns with renewed purpose and takes the stage at Glastonbury. While he fights to rebuild his career, can music save him again?

bbc-three.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
bbc-three.desc: A desolate train track seems the perfect spot to end it all – until someone else turns up with the same idea. Awkward.

bbc-three.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

bbc-three.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

bbc-three.title: The Catch Up
bbc-three.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

bbc-three.title: Deadly 60
bbc-three.desc: Steve Backshall is in Australia to search for the world’s most freaky and venomous fish: the stone fish. He also finds trapdoor spiders and a snake that hunts bats.

bbc-three.title: Deadly 60
bbc-three.desc: Steve Backshall is in the Kalahari to search for its weirdest residents. From aardwolves to aardvarks and mischievous meerkats, Steve is hoping for some Deadly firsts.

bbc-three.title: Sort Your Life Out
bbc-three.desc: Stacey and her team help a family sort out their clutter, helping to give their mum, who has limited mobility after an accident, her independence back.

bbc-three.title: Top Gear
bbc-three.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond, James May and the Stig return. Jeremy asks why there aren't more three-wheeled cars in the world and investigates further with a closer look at the 1980s comedians' favourite, the Reliant Robin. Meanwhile, James gives Top Gear's favourite warhorse, the Toyota Hilux pick up, one final assignment as he attempts to drive one up an active volcano in Iceland and the Stig rips up the track in the new Bentley Continental Supersports. Plus, Richard gives the old Chevrolet Lacetti a dignified send-off before he and Clarkson host a star-studded tea party to welcome in a shiny new Reasonably Priced Car.

bbc-three.title: 24 Hours with Ella Toone
bbc-three.desc: Unprecedented behind-the-scenes access to Manchester United and England star Ella Toone as she prepares for her wedding, while still coming to terms with the loss of her biggest fan, her dad.

bbc-three.title: Zara McDermott
bbc-three.desc: With access to luxury clubs, yachts and villas, as well as police, undercover detectives and emergency medics, Zara McDermott discovers the secrets of this unique island.

bbc-three.title: Zara McDermott
bbc-three.desc: Zara gets a closer look at how social media is changing Ibiza – and its crime.

bbc-three.title: Deepfake Porn: Could You Be Next?
bbc-three.desc: Most people think of deepfakes as manipulated video clips of world leaders, designed to create confusion, yet the most pressing threat of deepfakes is not politics, but porn. Research shows around 96 per cent of deepfake videos are pornographic, with almost 100 per cent of them involving non-consenting women. The film features three women who have been impacted by this form of image-based sexual abuse, including a leading Florida state senator, Lauren Book, UK campaigner Kate Isaacs and Dina, a woman who was deepfaked by a work colleague. We see the devastating emotional and psychological consequences the deepfakes have had for these women.

bbc-three.title: Brickies
bbc-three.desc: In this episode, three groups of brickies are on a housing development in Stoke-on-Trent. The brickies are under pressure to get the job done, but with a national shortage of materials, nothing is guaranteed. Jeorgia, from Wales, hopes to realise her dream of buying a sporty new car, but is dealing with the stress of working away from her home and family for the first time in her life. The site’s newest apprentice, Tom T, hopes to prove himself as he gets to grips with life in the real working world. And things take a turn for Tom C - the site’s labourer - when he unexpectedly finds himself out of a home and searching for somewhere to live.

bbc-three.title: Brickies
bbc-three.desc: The brickies are back in Stoke-on-Trent where things are falling behind schedule and a national mortar shortage threatens to grind production to a halt. Local bricklayer Patch has been drafted in to give the build an extra push. A young father of two, Patch has earmarked a forthcoming family holiday for the moment he hopes to finally ‘pop the question’ to his long-term girlfriend. After a falling-out at home, labourer Tom C is still searching for somewhere to stay. His workmates are there for him as he works through his options. He arranges to meet his ex- girlfriend as he looks to get things back on track. Meanwhile, after years of building homes for other people, Kuda is finally ready to buy his first dream house.

bbc-three.title: Brickies
bbc-three.desc: Newcomer Molly is used to working on smaller private jobs, but she’s now hitting the big time on a new housing development in Northampton. Describing herself as 'the only brickie you'll ever meet with hair extensions and fake eyelashes', she's larger than life. It’s the battle of the apprentices as the site’s young brickies, Lucas and Zac, go head-to-head and trade insults. Their rivalry begins at work but is settled on the foot golf pitch. Hard-working Kuda is hoping to hear back on his mission to buy his first house. The big boss visits the site and brings news - but it’s not what Kuda is expecting.

bbc-three.title: High
bbc-three.desc: Aged 19 and bored of where she grew up, Michaella craved sun and adventure. But her dream turned into a nightmare as she fell into an illicit world of drugs and excess.

bbc-three.title: High
bbc-three.desc: Michaella tells the story of finding herself 5,000 miles from home in Peru, pretending to be a tourist at Machu Picchu, then walking 12kg of cocaine into an airport.

bbc-three.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
bbc-three.desc: Brothers Steve and Stu scramble to put on a last-minute birthday show for Steve’s daughter. If they fail, Steve’s in the doghouse and Stu might end up... dead.

bbc-three.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

bbc-three.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

bbc-three.title: This is BBC Three
bbc-three.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

bbc-three.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
bbc-three.desc: Jobless is a sitcom all about the Adenugas, a middle-class British Nigerian family in Milton Keynes, in particular 16-year-old twin brother and sister Kehinde and Taiwo. Kenny is the family joker, who dreams of fame and fortune; sister Tai is, by contrast, sarcastic, super-smart and anxious to prove it. The twins live at home with their parents. Their mum, Bunmi, (chatty, energetic, control-freak tendencies, has a very stern side) runs Bunmi’s Beauty Salon, and despairs at her hapless offspring and their schemes to hit the big time. Tai and Kenny are desperate to make money by any means necessary – not only is financial independence very alluring, but the pressure they face from Bunmi doesn’t help. The twins’ different personalities inform their different approaches, however it seems neither of those personalities are quite compatible with the world of work.

bbc-three.title: Match of the Day Wales
bbc-three.desc: Live international football as Wales welcome World Cup-bound Ghana to the Cardiff City Stadium.

bbc-three.title: Louis Theroux
bbc-three.desc: Louis reflects on the criminal justice system in the USA, which has more prisoners than any other country in the world. In documentaries such as Behind Bars and Miami Mega Jail, Louis spent time with hundreds of incarcerated men, discovering a world with its own rules and codes and a system that seemed to be broken. In A Place for Paedophiles, Louis came face to face with those responsible for serious sex crimes against children who were being locked up indefinitely, even after their sentences had been completed. Louis also revisits his documentaries in Philadelphia and Milwaukee, where tensions between the African-American community and the police were already at boiling point, years before the recent killing of George Floyd.

bbc-three.title: Fake Friend: The Ticket Scammer
bbc-three.desc: How a Somerset high-flyer turned his charm and connections into tools of deception – conning his school friends and hundreds of others while living a life of glamour himself.

bbc-three.title: Top Gear
bbc-three.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond, James May and the Stig return. Jeremy asks why there aren't more three-wheeled cars in the world and investigates further with a closer look at the 1980s comedians' favourite, the Reliant Robin. Meanwhile, James gives Top Gear's favourite warhorse, the Toyota Hilux pick up, one final assignment as he attempts to drive one up an active volcano in Iceland and the Stig rips up the track in the new Bentley Continental Supersports. Plus, Richard gives the old Chevrolet Lacetti a dignified send-off before he and Clarkson host a star-studded tea party to welcome in a shiny new Reasonably Priced Car.

bbc-two.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
bbc-two.desc: As Saudi Arabia opens up to the outside world, miles and miles of its vast desert landscape remain completely unknown. Over 120,000 years ago, the Arabian desert was a pathway for the first human migrations out of Africa. On this expedition, Steve and the team are following in ancient footsteps to shine a light into the unknown. Joining forces with Saudi Arabian geologists Mahmoud Al-Shanti and Hala Alwagdani, Steve and ropes expert Aldo Kane head down into an unexplored volcanic underworld in search of the longest lava tube in Arabia. Geological evidence suggests that at least 400km of tunnels lie beneath the Harrat Khaybar lava field – subterranean passageways which could contain bones and artefacts from ancient civilisations. Only 25km of these lava tubes have ever been explored - until now. Steve and the team start their journey at the ancient Nabataean city of Hegra, where secrets lie waiting to be uncovered.

bbc-two.title: Half Man
bbc-two.desc: 2010. Niall and Ava face major life changes as Ruben struggles with his relationship. An impulsive choice between Mona and Niall has serious consequences for all involved.

bbc-two.title: Dear England
bbc-two.desc: After a breakthrough World Cup reignites belief, the England team return to a tournament on home turf, amidst the Covid pandemic. As the pressure to win increases, Pippa Grange questions her role. Meanwhile, Gareth tries to unite a divided country ahead of Euro 2020, where he hopes the team finally bring it home at Wembley.

bbc-two.title: This is BBC Two
bbc-two.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

bbc-two.title: Bargain Hunt
bbc-two.desc: Christina Trevanion visits a pub frozen in time, where last orders were called in the 1960s and everything was left untouched. In the Devon town of Buckfastleigh, the Valiant Soldier was an inn for more than two centuries and is now a museum, giving visitors a glimpse into the past. Inside the recreated interior, Christina is told about the pub’s redoubtable landlady and the custom back in the 60s of refusing service to women in the bar. In Hele, near Exeter, two teams of shoppers rush around an antiques centre with their experts, hoping to bag profitable bargains. On the red team are dad and daughter Ade and Mel, helped by Ben Cooper, and for the blues, it’s friends Martin and Mark, aided by Gary Pe. With 60 minutes on the clock, the blue team’s first stop is a shining suit of armour. Across the centre, Mel gazes into a £200 crystal ball – does it foretell a win for the reds? In Chippenham, auctioneer Tim Weeks confidently predicts a few profits for the teams, but who will go home the victor?

bbc-two.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
bbc-two.desc: Before boarding his train at Budapest’s historic Nyugati station, Michael Portillo explores its sumptuous royal waiting room, built specially for the Austro-Hungarian emperor Franz Joseph I and his empress, Elizabeth. Heading south, his next destination is the country’s third city of Szeged, close to the Serbian and Romanian borders. In the city’s historic centre, he finds the magnificent Great Synagogue, built in 1902, which reflects the size and prominence of Szeged’s Jewish population at that time. Just outside the city, on the banks of the River Tisza, Michael visits a region known for producing Hungary’s favourite spice, paprika, from ground red peppers. At a fourth-generation family farm, he tries a freshly picked pepper and joins the family for a hearty paprika stew.

bbc-two.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
bbc-two.desc: Carol Klein and Diarmuid Gavin, along with guest judge and gardening fan Fred Sirieix, continue the search to find Northern Ireland’s greatest garden. First up is Anne’s Kilcootry Barn Gardens in Fintona, with two and a half acres of mixed gardens – filled with flower beds, a vegetable garden and an apple orchard. In Omagh, the judges visit a field of dreams – 17 acres of once-barren farmland that’s been transformed by Sir Bob and Lady Rosemary, with a formal Italianate area and a large woodland, planted with wildlife in mind. Finally, it’s off to the shores of Rossglass beach, overlooking Dundrum Bay, to visit artist Bernard’s romantic rose garden. Judges Diarmuid, Carol and Fred are searching for a garden which the owners have put their heart and soul into creating. Which will they decide deserves to be the last contender for the title of Greatest Garden?

bbc-two.title: Just One Thing
bbc-two.desc: Can a sweet treat ever be good for you? Zoe Ball is in Birmingham, trying to tempt fitness fanatic Bilal away from sugary sweets and towards flavanol-rich dark chocolate. Chocolate may get a bad press, but the science shows that if you go over to the dark side and eat chocolate with at least 70 per cent cocoa, it could boost your heart health, improve your brain function and even reduce the risk of type 2 diabetes. A delicious upgrade with serious benefits.

bbc-two.title: BBC News
bbc-two.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

bbc-two.title: Politics Live
bbc-two.desc: The big political issues of the day.

bbc-two.title: Impossible
bbc-two.desc: The quiz show where players must avoid the Impossible answer to stay in the competition. 21 people begin the week, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 question?

bbc-two.title: Mastermind
bbc-two.desc: Another four contenders take to the black chair, answering questions on their chosen specialist subjects to earn a place in the semi-finals. Clive Myrie hosts an episode where the specialist subjects are Stephen Hawking, the music of Radiohead, Theda Bara and the Richard Sharpe novels of Bernard Cornwell.

bbc-two.title: Lightning
bbc-two.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

bbc-two.title: Clean It, Fix It
bbc-two.desc: Julie and her daughter Ruby had to downsize to their current home after her partner passed away, leaving them with a much smaller space to house all of their belongings. The team have one day to turn the loft space into a usable storage solution and reclaim space in Ruby’s bedroom and playroom.

bbc-two.title: The Great British Countryside
bbc-two.desc: Hugh Dennis and Julia Bradbury explore the most stunning parts of Cornwall and Devon to learn how Britain's past lives on in fascinating stories and characters.

bbc-two.title: Antiques Roadshow
bbc-two.desc: Michael Aspel presents the show in which members of the public are invited to bring along their antiques for examination. Finds at Shugborough Hall include a rare posset pot, a ceramic garden seat, an 18th-century chest, a Faberge brooch and a stay busk, an 18th-century corset, with a poetic message.

bbc-two.title: Flog It!
bbc-two.desc: Members of the public try to make money out of their antiques.

bbc-two.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
bbc-two.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Nikki Fox, Ruthie Henshall, Danny John-Jules and Ignacio Lopez take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

bbc-two.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
bbc-two.desc: On the last leg of his journey, Michael Portillo arrives in the historic northern city of Eger. At its famous castle, he discovers how, during the 16th century, Ottoman invaders were defeated by a vastly outnumbered Hungarian force. It‘s a victory still celebrated to this day. Nearby, in the foothills of the Bükk Mountains, one of Hungary’s wine-producing areas, Michael takes a tour of a family-owned vineyard and samples the region’s signature wine, Bull’s Blood. Continuing east, his final destination is the Puszta, Hungary's largest national park, where animals have grazed for over 2,000 years. On a vast plain with endless skies, Michael is treated to an impressive display of horsemanship by Hungarian cowboys, called csikós.

bbc-two.title: Digging for Britain
bbc-two.desc: Alice Roberts celebrates the biggest and best archaeological discoveries of 2018 from the north of the UK.

bbc-two.title: Springwatch
bbc-two.desc: Week two of Springwatch begins with another packed line-up of wildlife stories and live camera action from across the UK. Presenters Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan uncover the dramatic moments captured over the weekend by the live cameras at National Trust Crom in Northern Ireland. This programme explores the remarkable adaptations of some of Britain’s best-loved woodland birds, whilst Iolo Williams heads into the heart of London to reveal the unexpected wildlife thriving in the capital.

bbc-two.title: Emma Barnett: Fighting Endometriosis
bbc-two.desc: Emma Barnett shares her life-long struggle with endometriosis – a disease affecting one in ten women of reproductive age, which causes excruciating, often debilitating, pain. Inside Emma’s body, rogue cells similar to the lining of the womb mysteriously appear on other organs, creating lesions which bleed, scar and cause inflammation. There is no cure. Endometriosis is as common as diabetes but receives a fraction of the research funding. Emma travels across the UK to meet a few of the millions of women who, like her, are suffering. Through candid conversations, they reveal the extent to which endometriosis is devastating the lives of women – affecting fertility, work and loved ones.

bbc-two.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
bbc-two.desc: Paul and Bob travel to Dorset to fish the River Frome in search of grayling. They last fished for grayling successfully, but this time they have found a stretch of river known for setting the British record – so hopes are high that they could catch something big. As the pair fish along this unspoilt stretch of river, they realise grayling aren't the only species roaming these waters. And later, they attempt mackerel fishing on Chesil Beach, close to the species' feeding peak at dusk.

bbc-two.title: Newsnight
bbc-two.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

bbc-two.title: The Mother of All Cons
bbc-two.desc: In 2010, poorly 16-year-old Megan Bhari decides to launch a charity for seriously ill children. She’s battling a brain tumour, and knowing the toll illness can take, she decides to devote her life to creating fairy-tale experiences that might help children to forget, even briefly, that they’re sick. Believe in Magic becomes an immediate success. Inspired by Megan’s courage and self-sacrifice, celebrities and her beloved One Direction are more than willing to donate their time and money to the cause. And when her mother, Jean, posts news that Megan’s health has deteriorated and she needs expensive treatment in America, countless families and celebrity supporters rally around to support Meg. Together, they raise the money needed overnight. However, in an online chat, some parents whose own children have been treated for cancer abroad begin asking questions.

bbc-two.title: Iolo's River Valleys
bbc-two.desc: Iolo explores the stunning Dee valley, from wintering birds along the estuary to displaying black grouse on moorland, rare freshwater-pearl mussels and spawning sea trout.

bbc-two.title: Countryfile
bbc-two.desc: Matt Baker and Sammi Kinghorn explore the forests of the Welsh valleys at a critical time. As spring gives way to summer, wildlife is at its most active and visitor numbers rise, but wildfires pose a growing threat. They meet the people shaping the future of these landscapes, from fire prevention and recreation to conservation and training the next generation. Meanwhile, Tom Heap heads to Devon and Cornwall to investigate the state of the UK’s seaweed farming industry.

bbc-two.title: Race Across the World
bbc-two.desc: Six months after the race, all five teams reunite to relive their 12,000km journey from Palermo to Mongolia, with unseen moments and unprecedented behind-the-scenes access

bbc-two.title: Springwatch
bbc-two.desc: Springwatch returns for three weeks of seasonal splendour. Broadcasting live from the brand new location of National Trust Crom in Northern Ireland, Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan deliver all the wildlife drama spring has to offer. With remote cameras rigged across the site, they uncover stories unfolding in real time. Iolo Williams undertakes his own wildlife wanderings, broadcasting live from the Knepp Estate in Sussex, where wildlife is thriving and ‘stand-out’ species are returning to the landscape.

bbc-two.title: This is BBC Two
bbc-two.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

bbc-two.title: Bargain Hunt
bbc-two.desc: A hundred years ago in Yorkshire, it wasn’t ketchup that people added to their food - it was a delicious locally made relish. By the turn of the 20th century, 30 million bottles of Yorkshire Relish were being made in Leeds each year and shipped around the world. Made of vinegar, sugar, fruits and 27 spices, it was the go-to sauce for generations, right up until the 1950s. In the city where it was made, Danny Sebastian savours a collection of advertising items that celebrate this once-popular condiment. If that doesn’t whet your appetite, enjoy two teams of bargain hunters as they scour the antiques stalls at Wetherby Racecourse. On the red team are twins and classic car fans Mike and Joe, who hope to breakdance their way to victory - as long as they don’t break the bank first! Against them are mum and daughter Sue and Sarah, who plan on using their cha-cha-charm to get a good price. But which team will be dancing all the way to a profit at Caroline Hawley’s auction?

bbc-two.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
bbc-two.desc: On the last leg of his journey, Michael Portillo arrives in the historic northern city of Eger. At its famous castle, he discovers how, during the 16th century, Ottoman invaders were defeated by a vastly outnumbered Hungarian force. It‘s a victory still celebrated to this day. Nearby, in the foothills of the Bükk Mountains, one of Hungary’s wine-producing areas, Michael takes a tour of a family-owned vineyard and samples the region’s signature wine, Bull’s Blood. Continuing east, his final destination is the Puszta, Hungary's largest national park, where animals have grazed for over 2,000 years. On a vast plain with endless skies, Michael is treated to an impressive display of horsemanship by Hungarian cowboys, called csikós.

bbc-two.title: Farm 999
bbc-two.desc: In Leicestershire, Brandy, an elderly horse, is trapped in freezing mud on the banks of the Ashby canal. As the exhausted animal weakens, farmers and a specialist fire and rescue team join forces in a race against the clock to save him. With age and size working against him, the team make one final, desperate attempt to bring Brandy to safety, before it’s too late. In Lincolnshire, arson rips through a family farm, destroying buildings, animal enclosures and years of hard work. As the owners count the cost of the devastation, another farmer in Scotland recounts her own ordeal - watching her only tractor explode into flames after a petrol bomb attack. With deliberate farm fires on the rise, experts reveal how even a single act of recklessness can cause hundreds of thousands of pounds in damage. And in Hampshire, reporter Paul Colgan meets the innovators using drones to deliver defibrillators to the most remote corners of the countryside. By slashing emergency response times, this cutting-edge technology could transform the chances of survival and save lives on farms and in rural communities across the UK.

bbc-two.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
bbc-two.desc: The detectorists join forces with the freedivers to find not one but two family heirlooms lost by a heartbroken daughter. Glass artist Peter turns old, thrown-away bottles into stained-glass beauty, and archaeologist Sam looks into what could be our oldest find by our youngest treasure hunter.

bbc-two.title: BBC News
bbc-two.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

bbc-two.title: Politics Live
bbc-two.desc: The big political issues of the day.

bbc-two.title: Impossible
bbc-two.desc: The quiz show where players must avoid Impossible answers to stay in the competition. 20 people start today’s show, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 question?

bbc-two.title: Mastermind
bbc-two.desc: The hunt for the nation's Mastermind continues as Clive Myrie tests four more contenders on their specialist subjects and general knowledge under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock. The subjects in this episode are Marcus Trescothick, Rupert Brooke, Thomas Cromwell and the films of Wes Anderson.

bbc-two.title: Lightning
bbc-two.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

bbc-two.title: Clean It, Fix It
bbc-two.desc: Jan has lived in her three-bedroom house for over 30 years, but working seven days a week leaves her little time to clean and organise, and her collection of clothes and shoes are covered in dust. The team has just one day to turn her bedroom into a relaxing sanctuary and give her space to rediscover her love of fashion.

bbc-two.title: The Great British Countryside
bbc-two.desc: Hugh Dennis and Julia Bradbury venture across Britain's biggest county, Yorkshire, from the Moors and the Dales to the coast to discover how Britain's epic past lives on in the most fascinating stories and characters. Hugh takes the controls of a glider, and gets winched down into one of Britain's largest potholes on the end of a rope, past the tallest unbroken waterfall in Britain. Julia finds out how Yorkshire's rocks have turned Agatha Christie's handbag to stone, and how they produce mineral water. For a country the size of Britain, it has the biggest variety of geology on Earth, which has created not only a beautiful landscape, but also a fascinating industrial heritage, a rich history and many legends.

bbc-two.title: Antiques Roadshow
bbc-two.desc: Michael Aspel presents the show in which members of the public are invited to bring along their antiques for examination. This edition from Ramsgate in Kent features two teddy bears with their own x-ray plates from a dentist, a penny with a date which makes it worth a small fortune, a handbag made from an armadillo shell, and a valuable collection of Louis Wain cat pictures.

bbc-two.title: Flog It!
bbc-two.desc: This episode comes from the historic Blackpool Tower Circus. Paul Martin is joined by experts Anita Manning and James Lewis. Together the team pick out a selection of interesting antiques and collectibles to be sold at a local auction. James discovers some terracotta tomb figures that could be over 1,000 years old, and Anita gets her hands on a collection of seaside postcards. But the real star of the show is a well-preserved concertina. Paul also takes a look at one of Lancashire's most famous theatres, the Morecambe Winter Gardens, and tries his hand at some watercolour painting with renowned artist Geoff Butterworth.

bbc-two.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
bbc-two.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Nikki Fox, Ruthie Henshall, Danny John-Jules and Ignacio Lopez take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

bbc-two.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
bbc-two.desc: Michael Portillo’s Belgian rail journey continues in the Dutch speaking region of Flanders. In Antwerp, he discovers the city's historic multibillion-pound diamond industry and drops in at the workshop of an expert. Just outside the city, Michael boards the Dendermonde to Puurs Heritage Railway, run by volunteers, and gets a chance to drive one of its 1950s diesel engines. Heading to Belgium’s third city, Ghent, Michael visits the magnificent cathedral and marvels at a 15th-century Van Eyck masterpiece, the Adoration of the Mystic Lamb, the most stolen painting in the world.

bbc-two.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
bbc-two.desc: It’s fair to say that when it comes to classic national dishes, the French and British like to do things differently. However, both have a passion for poisson. Forty-five per cent of French households eat fish at least once a week, and Marcus wants to find out what kind of fish they like and if there’s a French dish to rival the great British fish and chips. If there's one fruit that might be even more synonymous with Provence than garlic, it's the olive. They’ve been grown here for over 3,500 years, and Marcus wants to understand the region’s passion for these tiny, versatile delights.

bbc-two.title: Springwatch
bbc-two.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan present the latest seasonal wildlife stories live from National Trust Crom, the stunning lakeside reserve cared for by the National Trust. Featuring part one of a special trilogy of short films examining the extraordinary relationship between the reed warbler and the common cuckoo, one of nature’s most fascinating examples of brood parasitism. Meanwhile, Iolo Williams broadcasts live from the Natural History Museum in London, where he turns the spotlight on the capital’s rich and often-overlooked invertebrate life.

bbc-two.title: QI XL
bbc-two.desc: Let Sandi Toksvig bring you to your senses with Aisling Bea, Roisin Conaty, Jessica Fostekew and Alan Davies.

bbc-two.title: Live at the Apollo
bbc-two.desc: The brilliant Ardal O’Hanlon introduces to the stage the amazing Mawaan Rizwan and the hilarious Sara Barron.

bbc-two.title: Newsnight
bbc-two.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Matt Chorley.

bbc-two.title: The Road to Hope with Fergal Keane
bbc-two.desc: The BBC’s Fergal Keane has spent 40 years witnessing the worst that humanity can do - genocide, war, and famine. So why is he full of hope? The answer lies in the extraordinary bravery he’s encountered in the worst places. In this powerful and humane film by Oscar-winner Alice Doyard, Fergal argues that despite the anxiety of the present we have real cause for hope.

bbc-two.title: Defiance
bbc-two.desc: Thriller based on a true story. In 1940s eastern Europe, four Jewish brothers flee to the forest to escape persecution and death at the hands of Nazi forces after their parents are murdered. Once there, they find more refugees are using the forest as a hideout, so they band together to share resources and attempt to outwit the German forces, who are always on their tail.

cartoon-network.desc: Meet the Wattersons: Mum, Dad, Anais, Darwin and of course Gumball… the 12-year-old cat with a flair for misadventure!

cartoon-network.title: The Amazing World of Gumball

itv.title: Millionaire Hot Seat
itv.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the exciting and fast-paced quiz where each episode sees six hopefuls compete for a one-million-pound prize - however, only one player can take their place in the Hot Seat at a time and answer multiple-choice questions to work their way up the Money Ladder. Unlike classic Millionaire, each question is against the clock and there is no room for indecision - if a player answers correctly, they remain in the Hot Seat and continue up the Money Ladder, but if they get a question wrong, they are eliminated, the Top Prize drops down, and the next player takes their place. Each player is given one Pass - a chance to pass the question on to the next player - but in doing so forfeit their place in the Hot Seat and risk not making it back there later on. Only one player will have a chance to win the top prize remaining on the ladder, and the player taking on that question must answer it correctly to take home the cash.

itv.title: The Vardys
itv.desc: At Jamie's final game for Leicester City, he scores his 200th goal for the club - it's a fairytale ending to one of the greatest underdog stories in sporting history. But he's not done with football yet, and the search for his new club begins. As speculation mounts, the Vardys consider offers from clubs all wanting to sign the 38-year-old striker. But none are in England, which forces the family to consider: would they go with Jamie, or stay at home? On deadline day, there is last-minute chaos as Jamie decides between two top clubs, one in Holland and the other in Italy. At the eleventh hour, he takes the Italian job. The Vardys are going to Italy! Determined to stay together as a family unit, Becky flies to Italy to find a house and schools for the kids - but in rainy Milan, nothing goes to plan.

itv.title: Torvill & Dean: The Last Dance
itv.desc: Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean reflect candidly on a remarkable 50-year career on ice as this fly-on-the-wall documentary captures every moment throughout their final UK and Australia tour and the prospect of an emotional 'Last Dance' - our cameras follow the Olympic legends behind-the-scenes throughout their final year as performers, from their preparations and final television performance on Dancing on Ice to the early gruelling training sessions and first rehearsals in Scotland, the iconic first show of the live tour in London, and then throughout the next energy-sapping four months as the duo tour with the support of an ensemble cast of world-class skaters, a unique show that tells their life story from their first meeting to their final-ever performance. There is every chance the wheels may come off, and this film captures the raw emotion of life on the road.

itv4.title: Die Another Day
itv4.desc: The 20th entry in the James Bond film series and Pierce Brosnan's final appearance as the secret agent. 007 is assigned to North Korea on a mission to assassinate a colonel when he is betrayed by a double agent at MI6 and captured - after enduring 14 months of torture, James is traded at the Korean border for Zao, a North Korean agent and associate of the colonel, in a prisoner exchange. Bond then escapes his subsequent MI6 detention and tracks Zao to Havana where he meets NSA agent Jinx and learns that she is working on the same case - James soon discovers that Zao is in league with the British diamond and technology tycoon Sir Gustav Graves... and that the two are planning to use a new satellite called Icarus to change the power balance of the world permanently.

nick-jr.desc: It is Mummy Pig's birthday, and Peppa, George and Daddy Pig are planning a surprise.

star-channel.title: O Homem do Norte
star-channel.desc: Do visionário realizador Robert Eggers chega O Homem do Norte, um épico cheio de ação que segue um jovem príncipe viking na sua demanda para vingar o homicídio do pai. Classificação etária: M/16.

star-channel.title: O Conselheiro
star-channel.desc: Determinado a subir na vida seja a que preço for, um advogado americano deixa-se envolver no perigoso mundo do tráfico de estupefacientes. Assim, aliciado pelo lucro fácil, parte para o México, onde faz negócio com Reiner, um poderoso barão da droga que tem em mãos um carregamento de cocaína avaliado em mais de 20 milhões de dólares. Classificação etária: M/16.

star-channel.title: Magnum P.I. T2 - Ep. 16
star-channel.desc: Quando Magnum e Higgins estavam em missão secreta como casal numa viagem romântica de autocarro, têm de entrar em ação quando um dos pombinhos do grupo é assassinado e as provas se deterioram rapidamente no calor do Havai. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Magnum P.I. T2 - Ep. 17
star-channel.desc: Magnum e Higgins lidam com o caso da recuperação da urna roubada do falecido marido de uma mulher, mas rapidamente descobrem que há quem ache que vale a pena matar por ela; Rick e TC confrontam um homem que se faz passar pela lenda do futebol, Andre Reed. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Magnum P.I. T2 - Ep. 18
star-channel.desc: À medida que o dia do seu casamento se aproxima, Magnum e Higgins ajudam uma Robin dos Bosques dos tempos modernos que, acreditando estar a ajudar uma escola a obter bens necessários, roubou drogas ilícitas sem saber. E ainda, Rick ajuda um soldado reformado com a difícil tarefa de voltar ao trabalho como civil. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Magnum P.I. T2 - Ep. 19
star-channel.desc: Magnum e Higgins são contratados por cada uma das partes de um casal que está num processo de divórcio litigioso, com o objetivo de descobrir os podres um do outro. Thomas e Higgins rapidamente se veem a competir pelos recursos disponíveis.

star-channel.title: Animal Control T2 - Ep. 8
star-channel.desc: Frank e Emily fazem um avanço importante no seu caso da rede de tráfico de animais exóticos. Shred e Rick desabafam sobre os seus problemas amorosos. Victoria e o pai de Patel beijam-se acidentalmente. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Evil T4 - Ep. 8
star-channel.desc: Através da visão remota, David descobre a vida de um homem que pondera cometer um homicídio seguido de suicídio, e a equipa apressa-se ao seu encontro. Sheryl executa o seu plano para impedir que o bebé Timothy se torne o Anticristo. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Hawai Força Especial T10 - Ep. 18
star-channel.desc: Enquanto a Five-0 investiga um homicídio num navio de carga onde os piratas se fizeram passar pela Guarda Costeira, Adam consegue finalmente as provas que necessitava para derrubar a operação da Yakuza de uma vez por todas. E ainda, Quinn ajuda Noelani quando ela suspeita que o tio não morreu de causas naturais. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Hawai Força Especial T10 - Ep. 19
star-channel.desc: A Five-0 investiga o caso de um fazendeiro que é assassinado depois de ter descoberto ossos humanos na sua propriedade, local onde, segundo a lenda, se enterraram moedas de ouro durante a Guerra Civil. Entretanto, a antiga enteada de Quinn, Olivia, pede-lhe ajuda, após o desaparecimento do seu pai. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.desc: Callen e a equipa tentam salvar uma criança em perigo iminente que sofreu uma lavagem ao cérebro por parte de uma célula terrorista. E ainda, Deeks e Kensi falam sobre a hipótese de terem um filho. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Investigação Criminal: Los Angeles T7 - Ep. 20
star-channel.desc: Enquanto destacados para proteger o comandante do Pacific Command, a equipa procura um espião norte-coreano. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: C.S.I. Miami T8 - Ep. 17
star-channel.desc: A equipa não tem qualquer dificuldade em encontrar suspeitos quando uma rececionista odiada é assassinada com um machado e o seu corpo é empurrado para uma conduta de elevador. A grande dificuldade é descobrir quem o fez. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: C.S.I. Miami T8 - Ep. 18
star-channel.desc: O filho de Horatio, Kyle, procura a ajuda do seu pai para resolver um assassinato. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Chicago P.D. T8 - Ep. 16
star-channel.desc: Voight e a equipa têm de pôr em prática todas as suas competências para derrubar uma rede criminosa implacável. Burgess corre perigo. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Chicago P.D. T9 - Ep. 1
star-channel.desc: Burgess luta pela vida. A Subcomissária Sam Miller está desesperada por encontrar o homicida do filho. Voight e Upton lidam com as consequências da sua decisão terrível. Ruzek lida com a pressão da situação. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Hawai Força Especial T10 - Ep. 16
star-channel.desc: No dia de São Valentim, Tani e Noelani ficam reféns durante um assalto a uma loja de conveniência. E ainda, a Five-0 investiga o assassinato de um diplomata tailandês, cuja mulher confessa o crime. Porém, Danny suspeita que a mulher está a encobrir a verdadeira identidade do assassino. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Hawai Força Especial T10 - Ep. 17
star-channel.desc: Harry Langford ajuda a Five-0 a investigar uma série de assassinatos, inspirados pelo argumento de um romance policial, não publicado, dos anos ‘20. E ainda, a amizade de Tani e Júnior sofre uma mudança significativa. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Investigação Criminal: Los Angeles T7 - Ep. 23
star-channel.desc: Um drama policial sobre o mundo arriscado do departamento policial e de investigação Office of Special Projects, uma divisão que está encarregada de apreender os mais elusivos e perigosos criminosos que possam ser considerados uma ameaça à segurança da nação. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Investigação Criminal: Los Angeles T7 - Ep. 24

star-channel.title: Magnum P.I. T3 - Ep. 1
star-channel.desc: Os novos clientes de Magnum e Higgins contratam-nos para encontrarem o irmão desaparecido, que foi visto pela última vez na ilha, o que leva a que Higgins seja alvejado e TC raptado quando um caso secreto de vingança é revelado durante a investigação. E ainda, Higgins (e Magnum!) ficam surpreendidos quando o cirurgião dela a convida para sair. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Magnum P.I. T3 - Ep. 2
star-channel.desc: O trabalho de Higgins de se reapoderar de um avião corre mal quando ela e Magnum descobrem que este estava a ser usado por um cartel de droga que abre fogo sobre o avião, forçando-os a fazer uma aterragem de emergência na selva, onde descobrem um clandestino a bordo. E ainda, Higgins tem o seu primeiro encontro com o Dr. Ethan Shah. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Hawai Força Especial T10 - Ep. 20
star-channel.desc: Enquanto a Five-0 investiga um esquema para roubar turistas, Tani ajuda Girard Hirsh a provar a inocência do seu tio idoso quando ele é suspeito de homicídio. E ainda, Tani e Júnior lidam com as circunstâncias de estar numa nova relação. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Hawai Força Especial T10 - Ep. 21
star-channel.desc: Depois de receber uma carta póstuma da mãe que contém uma cifra, Danny é atacado por alguém que quer roubá-la. E ainda, a Five-0 tem de ajudar Lincoln Cole (Lance Gross), um bom samaritano anónimo que está na lista negra de algumas pessoas muito perigosas. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Tracker T3 - Ep. 15
star-channel.desc: Randy pede ajuda a Colter quando um dos seus amigos mais próximos desaparece enquanto cumpre prisão domiciliária. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: FBI: Most Wanted T3 - Ep. 11
star-channel.desc: A task force procura um homicida que joga um jogo perverso do gato e do rato com as suas vítimas. Hana partilha algo da sua vida pessoal com Ortiz, o seu novo companheiro de casa. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: FBI: Most Wanted T3 - Ep. 12
star-channel.desc: A equipa procura um conhecido traficante de droga colombiano na sequência da sua fuga de uma prisão americana. Kristin começa a desabafar com o ex-marido sobre o seu passado. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.desc: A equipa viaja para a Síria para seguir um alvo de alta importância mas um dos elementos fica gravemente ferido na missão... Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Magnum P.I. T3 - Ep. 4

star-channel.title: Hawai Força Especial T10 - Ep. 22

star-channel.title: Hawai Força Especial T1 - Ep. 1
star-channel.desc: Ao regressar ao Havai para investigar o assassinato do seu pai, o Oficial da Marinha condecorado Steve McGarrett é recrutado pela Governadora para liderar uma equipa de ação de elite da polícia - as regras dele, o apoio dela, sem restrições. O primeiro caso envolve encontrar um traficante de armas relacionado com o homicídio do pai de McGarrett. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Sheriff Country T1 - Ep. 10
star-channel.desc: Com a vida de Travis em risco e o gabinete do xerife sob ataque, Mickey vê-se obrigada a fazer tudo o que for preciso para proteger a sua equipa. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-channel.title: Fire Country T4 - Ep. 10

star-channel.title: FBI: Most Wanted T3 - Ep. 13
star-channel.desc: A equipa tenta encontrar um homem de negócios procurado por homicídio e por um esquema de peculato. Jess aproveita o facto de Tali já não estar lá em casa para criar momentos românticos para Sarah. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-comedy.title: Um Corpo Perfeito
star-comedy.desc: Uma adolescente atraente e popular, cruel com os outros, vê-se no corpo de um homem mais velho e terá de encontrar forma de voltar ao seu corpo original.

star-comedy.title: A Máscara
star-comedy.desc: Stanley Ipkiss, funcionário de um banco, transforma-se num superherói maníaco quando usa uma máscara misteriosa. Classificação etária: M/12.

star-comedy.title: Seinfeld T2 - Ep. 1
star-comedy.desc: Após George terminar o relacionamento com a sua namorada, Jerry decide que a quer ver.

star-comedy.desc: Jerry teme que um comentário inapropriado que ele fez num jantar poderá ter causado a morte de um familiar.

star-comedy.title: Seinfeld T2 - Ep. 3

star-comedy.title: Seinfeld T2 - Ep. 4
star-comedy.desc: George deixa várias mensagens estranhas no gravador de chamadas da namorada e decide roubar a cassete.

star-comedy.title: Seinfeld T2 - Ep. 5
star-comedy.desc: Quando um apartamento fica livre no edifício onde Jerry mora, ele ajuda Elaine a conseguir ficar com ele, arrependendo-se de seguida. George começa a usar uma aliança de casamento pois ouviu que ajuda homens solteiros a engatar raparigas.

star-comedy.title: Seinfeld T2 - Ep. 6
star-comedy.desc: Uma estátua que George quer é roubada do apartamento de Jerry pelo namorado de uma escritora com quem Elaine trabalha atualmente.

star-comedy.title: Os Simpson T7 - Ep. 20

Depois, usa o cartão para alugar um carro e levar os seus amigos numa viagem. Entretanto, Lisa e Homer ficam mais próximos enquanto Homer trabalha na central nuclear e Marge tem de lidar com o facto de estar sozinha.

Entretanto, Homer destrói o seu último par de calças azuis e faz de tudo para convencer a fábrica a continuar a produzi-las.

Bart e Milhouse vão para a rua e entram em casa de Flanders onde destroem algumas coisas. Ned chama a polícia e os dois rapazes vão para a esquadra. Depois de alguns castigos e peripécias, os dois amigos percebem que a violência não leva a lado nenhum.

star-comedy.title: American Dad T6 - Ep. 7
